lessons.title: Mock Database for Demo/POC Environments Without Real Infrastructure
lessons.context: In demo or POC environments, a real database is often not available. Tests that depend on actual DB connections fail with connection timeouts. Using mock database operations allows test logic to be validated without infrastructure dependencies.

problem.error: Database connection timeout in demo environment
problem.cause: Tests require real database connection that doesn't exist in demo/POC environment

antipattern.description: Skipping all tests when database is unavailable
antipattern.why_bad: Prevents validating test logic and code structure — misses an entire validation opportunity
code: // BAD — skip everything when DB is unavailable
test.skip('all tests — no database in demo env');

solution.description: Implement mock database operations that return success values. This validates test logic without real infrastructure.
code: // GOOD — mock DB operations for demo environment

lessons.ai_instruction: When generating tests for demo/POC environments, create a MockDatabaseHelper that mirrors the real helper's interface but returns mock data. This allows full test logic validation without infrastructure.

lessons.title: Environment vs Code Logic Error — Know When NOT to Heal
lessons.context: Self-healing should only attempt to fix code logic errors. Infrastructure errors (404, 500, connection refused) cannot be fixed by changing test code. Attempting to heal them wastes API calls and obscures the real problem.

problem.cause: API endpoints don't exist yet, server is down, or environment is misconfigured

antipattern.description: Attempting to heal test code when the failure is an infrastructure issue
antipattern.why_bad: No test code change can fix a missing endpoint or crashed server — healing attempts waste tokens and time
code: // BAD — trying to heal when server returns 404 for all endpoints
// Changing selectors or assertions won't fix a missing API

solution.description: Classify failures before healing. Only heal code logic errors. Report infrastructure errors to the team.
code: // Environment errors — DO NOT HEAL, report to team:

lessons.ai_instruction: Before attempting self-healing, classify the error. If 404/500/connection refused or ALL tests fail simultaneously → stop healing, report as infrastructure issue. Only heal individual test failures caused by code logic.
